title: Liliths soul split across four bodies
display_title: Liliths soul split across four bodies
wikitext:
[]: [Spoilers] Lilith's soul split across four bodies?             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Thu May 05, 2011 10:05 pm I'm under the impression that Lilith's soul is split between her body in Terminal Dogma, Unit-00, Rei, and Unit-01. Some of this impression comes from supplementary material, like the game and the manga. The original body demonstrates limited sentience (not necessarily sapience) when it responds to Rei in End of Evangelion , telepathically communicating "welcome home" and then forming an orifice to allow Rei entry into its mass. In the PS2 game it demonstrates limited telepathy. Unit-01, during its frenzies, demonstrates similar behavior to Unit-00 during frenzy, and appears mainly to desire to acquire an S2 engine as soon as possible. In the manga, Unit-01 demonstrates a second personality, separate from Yui, that communicates with Rei and attempts to manipulate Shinji. Each of these personalities, I find, corresponds to the Neo-Pagan Triadic Goddess and the "three faces of Eve," the Maiden, the Mother, the Crone, and the Whore. Rei = Maiden (naive and ignorant of her situation) Terminal Dogma = Mother (loving and caring) Unit-00 = Crone (jaded and angry) Unit-01 = Whore (lusts after her desires) While unintentional, this is quite ironic, considering the symbolism behind the Lilith myth.

[]:             Quote Post by palehorseXIII » Fri May 06, 2011 12:49 am I do not think Unit-01 has any of Lilith's soul. And what about the Lilith myth?

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri May 06, 2011 2:15 am Yui is in Unit-1, so it's a three-way split.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Fri May 06, 2011 3:29 am Your theory might have bearing in the manga, but it doesn't affect the TV show canon. Just saying. Separate continuities.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri May 06, 2011 10:05 am EVA 01 has Yui in it, that is pretty clear. Also, it is possible that the lillith body was just reactong to the closeness of its soul (Kaworu could manipulate copies of Adam's body, after all) or that this stuff can be compared to the Rei clones turning their heads. Also, the manga is fanfic. Its author WAS one of the original crators, but he calls it his interpretation of anno's script. The "faces of Eve" motif IS almost omnipresent in EVA, tough, for example, there are three girls that represent the different qualities/sides of the female sex to Shinji and that he sort of lusts after. The Magi are almost a direct reference (Naoko as the mother, Naoko as the simple woman (seductress/whore, judging by the context that's given to it), and the scientist (crone/witch/wise woman)) I think I recently wrote a longer wall of text about it - the interesting bit about the girls in EVA is that, like real life ppl, all these aspects are parts of them/they do not completely follow one archetype and find themselves torn between them (The deliberately "unfitting" voice actresses reflect that) We see Shinji seeing them as one of this asapects and react disturbedly when he sees others in them. Misato is the whore/seductress trying to be the responsable person/mother/family member. Asuka is the child/maiden trying to be a seductress. Rei is created from teh mother archetypes of EVA, but Rei in her identity as Rei Ayanami is the fragile girl/Maiden. Ritsuko is a seductress that presents herself as the crone/witch. Mari may appear like a playful child outside of her EVA, but fully lives out her witch/keeper of knowdledge side once she enters an EVÁ (She was likened to a priestess chanelling the gods) Yui is a powerful character, a scientist, a warrior (old witch), but also yes, you guessed it, the nice, warm, welcomning mother. Maya is an innocent/helpless Maiden, but tries to be competent. (witch) Hikari... quite a minor character,but there is the side of her that's just an ordinary girl that wants top have friends and luvs Touji and the serious, bossy Hikari ("mother") (Shinji mostly percieves them as the latter and is reacts disturbed if he witnesses the former - in those he's interacted with, that is. He didn't really talk to Maya or Hikari and just met Mari - her advisor-like role to him suggests he's percieving her as the witch so far, but he didn't get to really witness the different sides of her. Also, Mari is Mari. She should be dissonant/at conflict like the others, but somehow she isn't. Mari is the unification of conflicting forces. ) Also, the Reis - Rei I is the child, of course, Rei II the "mother" as in, compared to the other Reis, nice, warm, laments being unable to have children, loyal... and the third one is the angry/"powerful" one, the "crone". As for the male characters, there's less paralelism there, there's sorta only one per age group. Shinji (young boy/adolscent), Kaji (young adult searching for partner) Gendo (older adult/father/"Lord") Fuyutsuki (midlife crisis ;) ), and Keel (old man/"prophet") I think Touji and Kensuke are, rather than other faces of "maleness" more other sorts of "heroes" - they have the two most common shonen Hero personalities (Exept they don't get "Chosen"/don't get heroic beginners luck) - Touji is the "tough, sort of rude, reliable protector" (Kurosaki Ichigo, Edward Elric, InuYasha etc...), Kensuke is the "Sorta naive, but good-natured big dreamer with an ambition" (Naruto Uzumaki, Ash Ketchum, Luffy... and anno's previous protagonist, Jean-Roque Raltique, whom Kensuke is a spitting image of) Kaworu is closer to your typical Rival character/ villain's-henchman-who-is-a-worthy-oponent than to any heroic archetype - (Teh bishieness, intellectual sort of person, the hero's exact oposite and mirror image at the same time... ) but also serves as a foil to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Fri May 06, 2011 12:37 pm Kendrix wrote: Kaworu is closer to your typical Rival character/ villain's-henchman-who-is-a-worthy-oponent than to any heroic archetype - (Teh bishieness, intellectual sort of person, the hero's exact oposite and mirror image at the same time... ) but also serves as a foil to Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri May 06, 2011 1:09 pm I'm not convinced that any of Lilith's soul remained in her original body. The black title dialogue cards never got assigned to specific characters before.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri May 06, 2011 1:12 pm He doesn't tell Gendo he sucks, he says something like "Oh, you're only human, too!" The "tragic death to create drama" is one important aspect, but he's also a foil. Shinji even lampshades it as the end, saying that Kaworu was both like Rei and like himself. They are a lot alike - they have some important mission to carry out (pilot EVA/cause TI) but they can't quite decide whether do do it or not, they like music and philosophy... They both came to nerv for their "mission", but are more interested in the people there (Shinji wants others to praise him/to keep the social circle he formed, Kaworu is interested in humans/ wants to get to know people he thinks are like him), they are artistic people.... But Shinji spends the fleeting days that remain brooding about the decision he has to make, while Kaworu makes the best out of them. Kaworu also has little problems with reaching out to others and reading them like books, he is charming, a bit like Shinji would want to be, what he could have been if he had seen himself like Kaworu sees him. He is not actually human, but yet he has all those qualities that make humans special and likes them - culture, free will... He is the sort of person that would die for an Ideal, as oposed to Shinji, who doesn't listen much to arguments and the like when he's in an emotionally charged state. He may seem like Shinji's exact oposite at first glance, but on the second, they're not that different - They're both nice and accepting - and eventually, both lack the courage to kill themselves. Kaworu has to ask Shinji to do it for him. In the end, Shinji cries over how Kaworu was better than him, but they weren't that different ans Kaworu did not think of Shinji as inferior - The irony is that Shinji loses the will to live here, after Kaworu died to give him a future, he depreciates himself for killing Kaworu, wen Kaworu sacrificed himself because he thought Shinji was great and was as fascinated of him as the other way around. Oh, and as for the "Welcome home", Rei is probably answering herself/ that dark, intangible part of hers welcomes the rest of her.l..

Some of this impression comes from supplementary material, like the game and the manga. The original body demonstrates limited sentience (not necessarily sapience) when it responds to Rei in En
